description: A fisheries biologist at the Minnesota Department of Natural Resources must analyze standardized gill net survey data from Lake Vermilion to produce the annual walleye stock assessment workbook. Using raw data from ~/Documents/Spreadsheets/gill_net_catches.csv (180 individual walleye records), ~/Documents/Spreadsheets/net_effort_log.csv (24 net lifts across 8 stations), and ~/Documents/walleye_standards.txt, create a comprehensive stock assessment workbook saved to ~/Documents/Spreadsheets/vermilion_walleye_assessment.xlsx. The workbook must include CPUE analysis (fish per net-night for each of the 8 stations and overall mean), a length-frequency distribution using 25mm bins from 200mm to 800mm, and Proportional Size Distribution (PSD and PSD-P) based on Gabelhouse length categories (Sub-stock <250mm, Stock 250-379mm, Quality 380-509mm, Preferred 510-629mm, Memorable 630-759mm, Trophy >=760mm). Additionally, calculate Relative Weight (Wr) for stock-length fish using the standard weight equation (Wr = (Actual Weight / Standard Weight) * 100) with a summary of mean Wr, provide an age-growth summary table showing mean length-at-age, and include a management summary section with a qualitative summary of the stock status and management recommendations based on the calculated metrics.
